_README: ILLUSTRATIVE SAMPLE decisions — NOT real TASCAT decisions. Citations are suffixed '(SAMPLE)' and provenance is 'SAMPLE'. Replace via ingest/decisions.py once outbound access to austlii.edu.au / tascat.tas.gov.au is enabled.

decisions.title: Applicant v Hobart City Council (SAMPLE)
decisions.summary: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Review of refusal of a discretionary visitor accommodation use in the General Residential Zone. The Tribunal weighed residential amenity (noise, traffic, transient occupancy) against the zone purpose.
decisions.principle: SAMPLE (paraphrased): In a residential zone, a discretionary visitor-accommodation use must demonstrate it will not unreasonably erode the residential amenity and character the zone exists to protect; unmanaged transient occupancy can justify refusal.

decisions.title: Applicant v Kingborough Council (SAMPLE)
decisions.summary: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Review concerning the adequacy of a bushfire hazard management plan for a single dwelling on a bushfire-prone lot. The accredited person's revised plan addressed defendable space, access width and static water supply.
decisions.principle: SAMPLE (paraphrased): A performance-based bushfire outcome can be accepted where a certified hazard management plan demonstrates tolerable risk; conditions securing the hazard management area and water supply are appropriate.

decisions.title: Applicant v Hobart City Council (SAMPLE)
decisions.summary: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Review of a dwelling exceeding the acceptable-solution height. Shadow diagrams and a streetscape analysis were assessed against the performance criterion, including a scenic skyline specific area plan.
decisions.principle: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Exceeding the acceptable height is permissible only where merit evidence (shadow diagrams, streetscape and view analysis) shows the performance criterion for amenity and scenic protection is met.

decisions.title: Applicant v Glamorgan-Spring Bay Council (SAMPLE)
decisions.summary: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Review of a refusal for development within a coastal erosion / inundation hazard band on the east coast. The coastal hazard report did not establish tolerable risk over the development's design life.
decisions.principle: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Within a mapped coastal hazard band, the onus is on the applicant to demonstrate tolerable risk for the design life; an inadequate coastal hazard report will sustain a refusal.
